acceptance_targets: [ch04-craft-001] At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:17 in the revision, Lin's scar is explicitly on the left side, and no later line in this chapter places or implies the scar on the right. A reader comparing the chapter to the continuity sheet finds one consistent side only.

[ch04-prose-001] Across the revised beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:91, :183, :343, :403, :427, and :453, no more than two use the `It wasn't/It was` antithesis. At least four of those six moments must land through materially different sentence shapes while preserving the same narrative meaning.

[ch04-prose-002] In the revised spans at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:149, :185, :233, :375, :391, and :447, at least four reaction turns register through touch, posture, breath, sound, or interior inference rather than `looked` or `eyes` as the lead verb. No more than two of those six beats may still rely on gaze as the primary reaction cue.

findings.acceptance_test: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:17 in the revision, Lin's scar is explicitly on the left side, and no later line in this chapter places or implies the scar on the right. A reader comparing the chapter to the continuity sheet finds one consistent side only.
findings.problem: Lin's signature scar is placed under the right ribs here, but the continuity sheet canonically identifies Lin's pale rib scar on the left side. Because this scar is the chapter's key identifying anomaly, the side swap is a direct continuity contradiction.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:17, change the scar placement to the left side and then re-check the nearby exam language so Mira and Tavi's handling still tracks the corrected anatomy. Preserve the scar's age, surgical neatness, and missing documentation; only the side should change.

findings.acceptance_test: Across the revised beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:91, :183, :343, :403, :427, and :453, no more than two use the `It wasn't/It was` antithesis. At least four of those six moments must land through materially different sentence shapes while preserving the same narrative meaning.
findings.problem: The chapter reuses the same antithetical emphasis pattern across multiple major beats: `It wasn't X. It was Y.` or near-variants. By the fourth and fifth occurrence, distinct emotional turns start landing in the same syntactic mold, which flattens prose that is otherwise much more alive than this fallback rhythm.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the turns at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:91, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:183, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:343, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:403, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:427, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:453 so only the strongest one or two keep the antithetical structure. Recast the others through action, image, or free-indirect thought that fits the specific pressure of each moment instead of reusing the same negation-then-definition beat.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised spans at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:149, :185, :233, :375, :391, and :447, at least four reaction turns register through touch, posture, breath, sound, or interior inference rather than `looked` or `eyes` as the lead verb. No more than two of those six beats may still rely on gaze as the primary reaction cue.
findings.problem: Emotional pivots keep defaulting to `looked`-led staging. That repeated gaze choreography becomes a prose tic, especially in a chapter whose style bible prioritizes touch, sound, and spatial pressure; too many turns are registered through who looks at whom instead of through more specific bodily or environmental cues.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the reaction beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:149, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:185, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:233, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:375, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:391, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:447 so at least four of them pivot through non-ocular cues: pressure in Mira's hands, Tavi's bench habits, Lin's anchoring motions, room heat/noise, or a sharper interior inference. Keep scene geography clear, but stop using eye direction as the default hinge.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into biotech grime, erotic awkwardness, debt language, bureaucratic absurdity, and the tenderness of people maintaining one another under bad systems. When choosing between an elegant abstraction and an ugly specific detail, choose the specific detail unless omission itself heightens pressure.
aesthetic_risk_policy.edge_case_resolution: Use restraint only when aftermath, gesture, residue, or changed behavior keeps the full pressure visible. Do not convert morally compromising acts into self-exonerating reflection, and do not flatten exploitative sex, poverty, or violence into generic dystopian mood.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches injury, sex, class shame, or institutional cruelty and the language retreats into tasteful fog, rewrite until the bodily fact, immediate sensation, and social consequence are fully legible on the page.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the norm in spoken English here; uncontracted forms are reserved for legal testimony, managerial performance, deliberate emphasis, or characters whose formality is itself a tactic.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate inspection-room procedural chapters with crowded comic clinic scenes, quiet domestic intervals, erotic pressure, warehouse horror, and civic hearing sequences; consecutive chapters should differ in scene density, dialogue load, and emotional temperature so the book keeps changing how it moves.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, urban, bodily, and unsentimental; technical vocabulary is welcome when embedded in action and cost, not as decorative futurism.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third-person close anchored to Mira Alves; the narration can infer social systems through what she notices, but it should not become omniscient exposition.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate crisp claim-note precision with longer, salt-wet, bodily sentences when feeling, memory, or city pressure breaches Mira's control.
